tasks.first_domino: Search warm March backpacking spots near Chicago
tasks.hook_line: Flight prices spike after this week
tasks.reward: Locks in the outdoor weekend — gear planning can start
nuggets: Zion 60-70°F mid-March, best warm option near Chicago
Big Bend Texas also warm, more remote
Need flights by this week to avoid price spike
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find warm backpacking destinations reachable from Chicago for March 14-15 weekend. Priorities: warm weather (60°F+), scenic trails, 2-3 hour flight max. Compare Zion, Big Bend, and Joshua Tree on weather, flight cost, and trail difficulty. I'm an intermediate hiker. Give me a recommendation with reasoning.

tasks.first_domino: Check Google Flights for March 14 departures
tasks.hook_line: Campsites fill up fast for spring weekends
tasks.reward: Trip is locked in — just need to pack
nuggets: Google Flights shows $120-180 RT to Las Vegas for Zion
Southwest has free cancellation if plans change
tasks.system_prompt: Help me book a backpacking trip from Chicago to Zion National Park for March 14-15. I need: cheapest flights (flexible on nearby airports), campsite reservation at Watchman or South Campground, and a 2-day trail itinerary for intermediate hikers.

tasks.first_domino: Save Fushimi Inari + Arashiyama to Kyoto doc
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples — which ones are worth the early wake-up?
tasks.reward: Closes out Kyoto planning — only Takayama and Tokyo left
nuggets: Fushimi Inari free entry, open 24hrs — best early morning
Arashiyama bamboo grove + monkey park half-day
Philosopher's Path connects Silver Pavilion to Nanzenji
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 2.5 days in Kyoto (March 25-27). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, walking, outdoor spaces, and good food. Already have hotels booked in central Kyoto. I want a day-by-day itinerary with opening hours, Google Maps links, and a suggested walking route. Include a mix of major landmarks and less touristy spots.

tasks.first_domino: Email insurance about Japan eyeglasses coverage
tasks.hook_line: One email now saves $400+ on 4 pairs of glasses in Japan
tasks.reward: Know exactly what's covered before shopping at Jins/Zoff
nuggets: Japan may need PD measurement on prescription
US insurance often reimburses overseas with receipts
Jins and Zoff in Kyoto do same-day glasses
tasks.system_prompt: Help me draft an email to my vision insurance (VSP) asking: 1) Does my plan cover eyeglasses purchased overseas in Japan? 2) What documentation do I need for reimbursement? 3) Is there a per-pair or annual limit? I'm planning to buy 4 pairs at Jins and Zoff stores in Kyoto.

tasks.hook_line: The tree provider is the spine — everything else plugs into it
tasks.reward: Tasks show up in the sidebar for the first time
nuggets: VS Code TreeDataProvider needs getChildren + getTreeItem
Can extend with drag-drop via TreeDragAndDropController
tasks.system_prompt: Help me implement a VS Code TreeDataProvider for a task management extension called Task Pilot. The tree should show tasks grouped by project, with each task showing its title, status (todo/in-progress/done), and priority. I need the provider class, tree item class, and registration in extension.ts. Use TypeScript.

recommended_slots.reason: 11 days out, flights need booking this week
Kyoto has no activity plan yet, 21 days out
Quick errand, low effort wind-down task
